tool_args.raw_text: This performance metrics document was prepared for the March 2026 meeting of the Bridges and Tunnels Committee.

payload.title: Bridges and Tunnels Key Performance Metrics

evidence_refs.source_quote: BRIDGES AND TUNNELS KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
March 2026
This performance metrics document was prepared for the March 2026 meeting of the Bridges and Tunnels Committee.
2 Broadway • New York, NY 10004 March 23, 2026

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: MTA Bridges and Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels (formerly the Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority/TBTA)

payload.description: MTA Bridges and Tunnels is comprised of over 1,000 employees and operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Bridges and Tunnels is comprised of over 1,000 employees and operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

tool_args.label: MTA Construction & Development
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development continues to make strong progress on a design-build contract for upgrades of several critical systems at the Queens suspended span of the Robert F. Kennedy (RFK) Bridge.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development continues to make strong progress on a design-build contract for upgrades

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people

tool_args.label: Robert F. Kennedy Bridge Queens Span Upgrades
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development continues to make strong progress on a design-build contract for upgrades of several critical systems at the Queens suspended span of the Robert F. Kennedy (RFK) Bridge. The $410 million, 3 ½-year contract was awarded in December 2023.

payload.project_name: Robert F. Kennedy Bridge Queens Span Upgrades
payload.description: Upgrades including structural steel repairs, ADA-compliant shared-use path, main cable dehumidification system, and Weigh-In-Motion system

evidence_refs.source_quote: Robert F. Kennedy Bridge Queens Span Upgrades

MTA Construction & Development continues to make strong progress on a design-build contract for upgrades of several critical systems at the Queens suspended span of the Robert F. Kennedy (RFK) Bridge. The $410 million, 3 ½-year contract was awarded in December 2023.

tool_args.label: February 2026 total traffic decrease vs Feb 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels in February 2026 was lower by a total of 1,251,224 vehicles or 5.2%, compared to February 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels in February 2026 was lower by a total of 1,251,224 vehicles or 5.2%, compared to February 2025.

tool_args.label: YTD Feb 2026 total traffic decrease vs YTD Feb 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Year-to-date traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels through February 2026 was lower by a total of 2,233,723 vehicles or 4.5%, compared to year-to-date February 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Year-to-date traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels through February 2026 was lower by a total of 2,233,723 vehicles or 4.5%, compared to year-to-date February 2025.

tool_args.label: CBD entries YTD through Feb 2026
tool_args.raw_text: Year-to-date through February 2026, entries into the Central Business District (CBD), which includes the Congestion Relief Zone (CRZ) and Excluded Roadways, totaled 27,819,889 vehicles, averaging 471,524 vehicles per day. Of these, 24,743,484 were CRZ entries, averaging 419,381 vehicles per day.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Year-to-date through February 2026, entries into the Central Business District (CBD), which includes the Congestion Relief Zone (CRZ) and Excluded Roadways, totaled 27,819,889 vehicles, averaging 471,524 vehicles per day. Of these, 24,743,484 were CRZ entries, averaging 419,381 vehicles per day.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: CRZ entries YTD through Feb 2026
tool_args.raw_text: 24,743,484 were CRZ entries, averaging 419,381 vehicles per day.

payload.raw_value_text: 24,743,484 CRZ entries, averaging 419,381 vehicles per day

evidence_refs.source_quote: Of these, 24,743,484 were CRZ entries, averaging 419,381 vehicles per day.

tool_args.label: Average daily CBD and CRZ entries decrease vs 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Average daily CBD and CRZ entries were 12.3% lower than during the same period in 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Average daily CBD and CRZ entries were 12.3% lower than during the same period in 2025.

tool_args.label: Total collision rate February 2026
tool_args.raw_text: In February 2026, MTA Bridges and Tunnels recorded a total collision rate of 2.3 per million vehicles, a 16.2% decrease from the rate of 2.8 per million vehicles recorded in February 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In February 2026, MTA Bridges and Tunnels recorded a total collision rate of 2.3 per million vehicles, a 16.2% decrease from the rate of 2.8 per million vehicles recorded in February 2025.

tool_args.label: Collisions-with-injury rate February 2026
tool_args.raw_text: The collisions-with-injury per million vehicles also declined to 0.6 per million vehicles, a 28.4% reduction from last year's rate of 0.8.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The collisions-with-injury per million vehicles also declined to 0.6 per million vehicles, a 28.4% reduction from last year's rate of 0.8.

tool_args.label: Lost-time injury rate January 2026
tool_args.raw_text: The lost-time injury rate for January 2026 was 3.2, which constitutes a 30.2% decrease relative to January 2025, when the rate was 4.5.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The lost-time injury rate for January 2026 was 3.2, which constitutes a 30.2% decrease relative to January 2025, when the rate was 4.5.

tool_args.label: Average incident response time 2026
tool_args.raw_text: The average incident response time for 2026 was 6.3 minutes, representing a 1.4% improvement from 6.4 minutes in February 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The average incident response time for 2026 was 6.3 minutes, representing a 1.4% improvement from 6.4 minutes in February 2025.

tool_args.label: Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge traffic decrease Feb 2026
tool_args.raw_text: The Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge had the largest reduction, with 335,340 fewer vehicles than in February 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge had the largest reduction, with 335,340 fewer vehicles than in February 2025.

tool_args.label: Roadway interdictions February 2026
tool_args.raw_text: During February 2026, enforcement efforts across MTA Bridges and Tunnels resulted in the interdiction of 172 vehicles for persistent toll violations and the issuance of more than 2,700 summonses

evidence_refs.source_quote: During February 2026, enforcement efforts across MTA Bridges and Tunnels resulted in the interdiction of 172 vehicles for persistent toll violations

tool_args.label: Summonses February 2026
tool_args.raw_text: the issuance of more than 2,700 summonses—the highest February summons activity in the past five years

evidence_refs.source_quote: the issuance of more than 2,700 summonses—the highest February summons activity in the past five years

tool_args.label: Overweight truck annual damage cost
tool_args.raw_text: Overweight trucks—estimated to be 6.5% of trucks that cross the Agency's spans—cause damage to our bridge structures, increasing the need for maintenance and repairs, as well as long-term construction costs—approximately $30 million annually.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Overweight trucks—estimated to be 6.5% of trucks that cross the Agency's spans—cause damage to our bridge structures, increasing the need for maintenance and repairs, as well as long-term construction costs—approximately $30 million annually.

tool_args.label: Toll revenue YTD variance
tool_args.raw_text: Year-to-Date toll revenue is $2.3 million below the Adopted Budget, primarily due to winter weather.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Year-to-Date toll revenue is $2.3 million below the Adopted Budget, primarily due to winter weather.

tool_args.label: CRZ net surplus YTD
tool_args.raw_text: Year-to-Date CRZ revenue is favorable to budget by $13.5 million, with a year-to-date net surplus of $96.1 million.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Year-to-Date CRZ revenue is favorable to budget by $13.5 million, with a year-to-date net surplus of $96.1 million.

tool_args.label: RFK WIM system installation completed
tool_args.raw_text: Installation of a new Weigh-In-Motion (WIM) system, a state-of-the-art technology that enables MTA Bridges and Tunnels to weigh vehicles in real time as they cross the bridge and automatically issue tickets to overweight trucks for direct enforcement, was completed in late 2025, with the program scheduled to go live in late 2026.

payload.description: Installation of Weigh-In-Motion (WIM) system at RFK Bridge completed

evidence_refs.source_quote: Installation of a new Weigh-In-Motion (WIM) system...was completed in late 2025

tool_args.label: Winter Storm Hernando travel ban
tool_args.raw_text: Winter Storm Hernando, which included a travel ban on all non-essential vehicles on streets, highways, bridges and tunnels from 9 PM (7 PM for trucks) on February 22nd through noon on February 23rd.

payload.description: Travel ban on all non-essential vehicles from 9 PM (7 PM for trucks) February 22 through noon February 23 due to Winter Storm Hernando
